title: Project reconciliation candidates
description: Return a deterministic provider-neutral candidate projection for approved statement evidence without mutating Ledger state. Implements G10 and UC-LEDGER-014 under A14 and C20.
rationale: INGEST needs a stable match-first contract, while LEDGER must expose and validate candidate facts rather than guess from provider wording.
story_want: inspect compatible unreconciled candidates for statement evidence
story_so_that: strong, absent, and ambiguous outcomes can be distinguished before any match is applied
acceptance_criteria: Given approved statement-row evidence, canonical candidate facts, an owned account, and a statement scope, when reconciliation candidates are requested, then the Ledger returns only active compatible unreconciled transactions with stable identifiers and explicit compatibility or exclusion reason codes.

Given zero, one, or multiple compatible candidates, when the projection is returned, then it classifies the outcome as no-candidate, unique-candidate, or ambiguous respectively without registering a reconciliation decision, linking evidence, or creating a transaction.

Given a voided or superseded transaction, mismatched account or currency, incompatible sign or amount semantics, or conflicting active statement evidence, when projection runs, then that transaction cannot be returned as a deterministic match candidate.

Given unchanged Ledger state, identical candidate facts, scope, and deterministic-policy identity, when projection is repeated, then candidate membership, ordering, facts, and reason codes are identical.

Given provider-specific transport, sender, mailbox, MIME, recipient, schedule, or delivery fields, when projection input is validated, then the Ledger rejects them before any state access or mutation.
security_criteria: The projection returns only the privacy-safe canonical and evidence metadata required to explain compatibility; it never returns provider payloads or full account or instrument identifiers.
